name: San Salvador de Cantamuda and Requejada Reservoir Loop
name_original: Los Valles Oseros — circular por Parque Natural Montaña Palentina
description: Tackle the difficult 24.7-mile Los Valles Oseros mountain biking loop in Parque Natural Montaña Palentina, featuring 3807 feet of elevation
region_intro: Mountain bike trails Cervera De Pisuerga traverse the Palencia mountain range, characterized by mountainous terrain, rivers, and valleys. The region serves as a primary entry point to the Parque Natural Montaña Palentina, offering dramatic mountains, lush valleys, and serene reservoirs. This varied landscape provides diverse routes for mountain biking, including paths through the Cantabrian Mountains. Best mountain bike trails around Cervera De Pisuerga The most popular mountain bike trail is Senda del Oso, a 8.4 miles (13.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 21 minutes to complete. This route features an elevation gain of 1,122 feet (342 meters). Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Vuelta de la Embalsa de la Ruesga — Parque Natural Montaña Palentina, a moderate 12.0 miles (19.4 km) path. This route circles the Ruesga Reservoir within the natural park, offering views of the water and surrounding mountains. Local mountain bikers also love the San Salvador de Cantamuda and Requejada Reservoir Loop, a 24.7 miles (39.8 km) trail leading through the Montaña Palentina landscape, often completed in about 4 hours 7 minutes. Mountain biking around Cervera De Pisuerga is defined by mountainous terrain, river valleys, and natural park landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Cervera De Pisuerga are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from more than 40 reviews. More than 160 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Cervera De Pisuerga's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Cervera De Pisuerga?
A: There are over 30 mountain bike trails around Cervera De Pisuerga, offering a variety of options for different skill levels. These routes traverse the mountainous terrain, river valleys, and natural park landscapes of the region.

Q: What is the best season for mountain biking in Cervera De Pisuerga?
A: The Palencia mountain range, where Cervera de Pisuerga is located, generally offers pleasant conditions for mountain biking from spring through autumn. Summer provides warm, dry weather, while spring and autumn bring cooler temperatures and vibrant scenery, ideal for longer rides. Always check local weather conditions before heading out.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Cervera De Pisuerga?
A: The mountain bike trails in Cervera De Pisuerga are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 160 mountain bikers have explored the varied terrain, often praising the scenic views and diverse landscapes within the Parque Natural Montaña Palentina.

Q: Are there easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Cervera De Pisuerga?
A: Yes, there are a few easy mountain bike trails in Cervera De Pisuerga. These routes are generally less demanding in terms of elevation gain and technical difficulty, making them suitable for beginners or those looking for a relaxed ride. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1153412477/senda-del-oso" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda del Oso&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate trail that can be enjoyed by riders with some experience.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Cervera De Pisuerga offers 6 difficult mountain bike trails for experienced riders seeking a challenge. These routes often feature significant elevation changes and more technical sections. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1175786946/san-salvador-de-cantamuda-and-requejada-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Salvador de Cantamuda and Requejada Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, is a challenging 24.7-mile route with over 1,100 feet of elevation gain.

Q: Can I find long-distance mountain biking routes in the area?
A: Yes, the region provides options for longer mountain biking adventures. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1175846291/valles-de-muda-y-la-castilleria-circular-por-parque-natural-montana-palentina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valles de Mudá y la Castillería — circular por Parque Natural Montaña Palentina&lt;/a&gt; span over 28 miles, offering extensive exploration of the natural park's diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes around Cervera De Pisuerga?
A: Many mountain bike routes in Cervera De Pisuerga are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1153477891/vuelta-de-la-embalsa-de-la-ruesga-parque-natural-montana-palentina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vuelta de la Embalsa de la Ruesga — Parque Natural Montaña Palentina&lt;/a&gt;, which circles the scenic Ruesga Reservoir, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1178411108/san-salvador-de-cantamuda-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Salvador de Cantamuda Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What scenic views can I expect on mountain bike trails in Cervera De Pisuerga?
A: Mountain bike trails in Cervera De Pisuerga offer breathtaking scenic views, particularly within the Parque Natural Montaña Palentina. You can expect panoramic vistas of dramatic mountains, lush valleys, and serene reservoirs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/586766" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Piedrasluengas Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, provides stunning views of the Picos de Europa and surrounding peaks, making it a rewarding cycling destination.

Q: Are there any waterfalls accessible by mountain bike in Cervera De Pisuerga?
A: Yes, the region features natural attractions like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3487275" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mazobre Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which can be incorporated into mountain biking routes, offering a refreshing stop amidst your ride.

Q: What natural landmarks or attractions can I explore while mountain biking?
A: Beyond the trails, you can explore several natural landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/586766" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Piedrasluengas Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; offers expansive mountain views. The Ruesga Reservoir is a scenic spot for a break. You might also encounter impressive natural features like El Roblón de Estalaya, an 800-year-old oak tree, or the Tejeda de Tosande and Fossil Forest areas.

Q: Are there historical or cultural sites to visit near the mountain bike trails?
A: Cervera de Pisuerga and its surroundings are rich in history. You can visit the 16th-century Church of Santa María del Castillo in town or explore the Ethnographic Museum Isla Piedad. The Hermitage of San Vicente cave, an early medieval rock-cut site, is also a unique historical point of interest in the region, which boasts one of Europe's largest concentrations of Romanesque architecture.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for families in Cervera De Pisuerga?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations for mountain biking can vary, the region's easier and moderate trails, particularly those with less technical terrain and gentler gradients, can be suitable for families with some cycling experience. The scenic paths around the Ruesga Reservoir, for example, offer a pleasant environment for a family outing.
tips: Rocky wall where you can see the impression made by the tree trunks more than 300 million years ago.

Current parish church of San Salvador de Cantamuda in the Natural Park of the Palencia Mountain, dating from the 12th century.
It has a very pretty western façade with a spectacular Romanesque-style belfry with a double hole for bells.
The Requejada Reservoir is formed by the Pisuerga River reservoir. From here you can see the entire other side of the lake and the forest that surrounds it.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Los Valles Oseros mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This route is considered difficult, both physically and technically. It demands very good physical fitness and advanced riding skills. You should be prepared for significant ascents and technical sections.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and demanding, predominantly featuring wide mountain paths and singletracks. Approximately 70% consists of tracks, 20% singletracks, and 10% asphalt sections. You will encounter unpaved and difficult segments, making a mountain bike with generous tire volume ideal.

faqs.question: What natural features and landmarks can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers spectacular scenery with imposing limestone massifs, deep valleys, and lush forests. You'll pass by notable points such as the Fossil Forest Cliff and the Collegiate Church of San Salvador de Cantamuda. There are also stunning views, including the View of the Requejada Reservoir and the View of Embalse de la Requejada, with glimpses of the Palentina mountain range and sometimes even the distant Picos de Europa.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I encounter while mountain biking in Parque Natural Montaña Palentina?
faqs.answer: The Montaña Palentina Natural Park is a crucial habitat for various wildlife. You might have the opportunity to spot endangered Cantabrian brown bears, deer, wild boars, raptors, wolves, Cantabrian chamois, wild cats, golden eagles, griffon vultures, eagle owls, and falcons.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The recommended seasons for mountain biking this trail are May, June, July, September, and the first half of October. It's best to avoid periods of heavy rain and snow, especially at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Los Valles Oseros mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: As this is a circular route within the Parque Natural Montaña Palentina, you can typically find parking in nearby villages or designated areas within the park. Specific trailhead parking information is not provided, but starting points in towns like Cervera de Pisuerga or Velilla del Río Carrión are common for routes in the area.

faqs.question: Is the Los Valles Oseros mountain bike trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Montaña Palentina Natural Park generally allows dogs, it's crucial to keep them on a leash, especially given the presence of significant wildlife, including endangered species like the Cantabrian brown bear. Always ensure your dog is under control to protect both them and the local fauna.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to mountain bike in Parque Natural Montaña Palentina?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking on public trails within the Parque Natural Montaña Palentina. However, always respect park regulations, stay on marked paths, and be mindful of environmental protection rules.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. You will find sections that overlap with the Asturian Way, Senda del Oso, Sendero Histórico: Palencia, Senda del Roblón, Senda del Bosque Fósil, and Camino de Sopeña.

faqs.question: What type of bike is most suitable for this route?
faqs.answer: Given the varied and demanding terrain, a mountain bike is ideal for this route, particularly one with generous tire volume to handle the technical and unpaved segments effectively.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation near the trail?
faqs.answer: The Montaña Palentina region is less populated, offering a tranquil experience. However, you can find amenities such as cafes, pubs, and accommodation in nearby towns like Cervera de Pisuerga, Guardo, Velilla del Río Carrión, and Aguilar de Campoo, which serve as good bases for exploring the park.
